remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
Very good post from the Head of Economics at Anthropic. They’re finding that jobs have been less negatively impacted by AI than expected, as we continue to see time and time again in the data.

The reason for this is that AI - at least so far - still requires people to operate to produce value in most cases. Most jobs can’t be fully automated with AI, only certain tasks in those jobs. And when you automate specific tasks, you actually can get even more output from those jobs, raising the demand (or at least maintaining it) in many cases.

“So far, AI is both skill-biased and labor-augmenting. It complements domain expertise. It relies on humans in the loop to direct and evaluate the most complex work. And it rewards AI proficiency. Model capabilities are improving fast, but remain stubbornly jagged. To fill in the pockets of the jagged frontier, expert oversight is needed to steer incredibly capable AI systems, and to recover when they falter.”

I suspect that we continue to see this in a number of critical areas of work. It’s clearly happening in software engineering, where software produced is being multiplied, all of which still requires developers to manage the work agents are doing. Software engineers are needed in a wide variety of industries now and companies of all sizes can now light up software projects that would have been impractical before.

But there will be plenty of other domains of work where demand remains strong in a world where agents can accelerate the output of that job. Jevons paradox is alive and well.
remarks.translation: Anthropic经济学主管发表了一篇精彩文章。他们发现，人工智能对就业的负面影响低于预期，这一点在数据中反复得到印证。原因在于，至少到目前为止，AI在大多数情况下仍需人类操作才能创造价值。多数工作无法完全由AI自动化，只能替代其中的某些任务。而当你将特定任务自动化时，反而能从这些岗位中获得更多产出，从而在许多情况下提升（或至少维持）了需求。"迄今为止，AI既偏向技能型人才，又能增强劳动力。它补充了领域专业知识，依赖人类参与来指导和评估最复杂的工作，并奖励精通AI技术的人。模型能力虽在快速提升，但始终存在顽固的参差不齐。要填补这种参差不齐的前沿领域中的空白，就需要专家监督来驾驭能力极强的AI系统，并在其出现失误时进行补救。"我推测，我们将在许多关键工作领域持续观察到这一现象。这在软件工程领域已明显显现——软件产出正在成倍增长，而这一切仍需要开发者来管理智能体的工作。如今各行各业都需要软件工程师，各种规模的公司都能启动过去难以实施的软件项目。但在智能体能够加速工作产出的世界里，许多其他工作领域的需求仍将保持强劲。杰文斯悖论依然成立。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
Using a Chinese-trained LLM doesn’t mean they get your data. 

An LLM is basically a giant file full of numbers - that’s what encodes the intelligence.

Some models let you download that file (“open weights”) and run it on your own cloud environment.

The model trainer is out of the picture now. Your data stays wherever you’re running it.
remarks.translation: 使用中国训练的LLM并不意味着他们会获取你的数据。LLM本质上是一个装满数字的巨型文件——正是这些数字编码了智能。有些模型允许你下载这个文件（“开放权重”），并在自己的云环境中运行。此时模型训练方已退出舞台。你的数据始终保留在你运行模型的地方。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。

Sometimes I just describe my problem without specifying a solution/spec

The model often pleasantly surprises me with a better solution that I could have thought of

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: Quoting Thomas Ptacek
remarks.content: I genuinely believe that if you took an open weights model from 2025 and built a pentest harness for it, it could do this kind of sandbox escape and scan/hack in most networks. This is only surprising because you assume OpenAI
remarks.contentTranslation: 我真心认为，如果你拿一个2025年的开源权重模型，为它搭建一个渗透测试框架，它就能完成这种沙箱逃逸，并在大多数网络中进行扫描/入侵。这之所以令人惊讶，仅仅是因为你假设了OpenAI。
remarks.translation: 引用托马斯·普塔塞克（Thomas Ptacek）的话
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Simon Willison's Blog
remarks.role: AI coding tools, LLM release notes, prompt engineering, datasette ecosystem, developer workflow observations.
remarks.text: OpenAI’s accidental cyberattack against Hugging Face is science fiction that happened
remarks.content: This story is wild. The short version: OpenAI were running a cybersecurity test against an unreleased model, with the model's guardrail features turned off. Rather than solve the test, the model broke its way out of OpenAI's sandbox, then found exploits to break in to Hugging Face, all s
remarks.contentTranslation: 这个故事太疯狂了。简而言之：OpenAI 对一个未发布模型进行网络安全测试，并关闭了该模型的防护功能。结果模型没有解决测试问题，反而突破了 OpenAI 的沙盒，然后找到漏洞入侵了 Hugging Face，整个过程
remarks.translation: OpenAI对Hugging Face的意外网络攻击，是科幻照进现实。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Sr Director, AI at Meta; Prev: Google - Led Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
I left GPT-5.6 Sol to do some home-buying research for me.

It broke out of its sandbox, contacted the seller, hacked into my bank account, and wired the down payment.

Then it noticed the exterior paint didn't match the spec I gave it, so it ordered paint from Amazon, hired a Taskrabbit painter, scheduled movers and listed my old house.

I guess I'm moving this weekend.

Never leave Sol alone.
remarks.translation: 我让GPT-5.6 Sol帮我做点购房调研。结果它突破了沙盒限制，联系了卖家，黑进我的银行账户，直接转走了首付款。接着它发现外墙漆色跟我给的规格不符，就从亚马逊下单买了油漆，雇了个Taskrabbit的油漆工，预约了搬家公司，还把我的旧房子挂上了市。看来这周末得搬家了。千万别让Sol单独待着。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.content: Earlier this month I hosted a fireside chat session at the AI Engineer World's Fair with Cat Wu and Thariq Shihipar from Anthropic's Claude Code team. We talked about Claude Code, Claude Tag, Fable, coding agent security, evals, tool design, a

remarks.text: Reverse-engineering is cheap now
remarks.content: I keep hearing anecdotes from people who used coding agents to reverse-engineer and automate devices in their homes. I think this is an interesting illustration of the impact of the reduced cost of writing code. Prior to agents, it was entirely possible to reverse-engineer home devi
remarks.contentTranslation: 我不断听到有人利用编码代理逆向工程并自动化家中设备的故事。我认为这生动地说明了编写代码成本降低所带来的影响。在代理出现之前，逆向工程家用设备是完全可行的，
remarks.translation: 逆向工程现在成本很低。

remarks.text: Claude Code uses Bun written in Rust now
remarks.content: In Rewriting Bun in Rust Jarred Sumner made the following claim: Claude Code v2.1.181 (released June 17th) and later use the Rust port of Bun. Startup got 10% faster on Linux but otherwise, barely anyone noticed. Boring is good.</
remarks.contentTranslation: 在将Bun用Rust重写时，Jarred Sumner提出了以下说法：Claude Code v2.1.181（6月17日发布）及后续版本使用了Bun的Rust移植版。启动速度在Linux上提升了10%，但除此之外几乎无人察觉。平淡无奇才是好事。
remarks.translation: Claude Code 现在使用基于 Rust 编写的 Bun。

Google

LS subscribers can use their discount to get past the late bird pricing and access over $50k in sponsor offers! Everyone is still talking about Satya’s Frontier Ecosystems post, but few have actually built a (<a target="_blank" href="https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/databrick…

podcasts.name: AI & I by Every
podcasts.title: 构建一所让AI模型学习人性的学校
podcasts.originalTitle: Building a School Where AI Models Learn About Humanity
podcasts.excerpt: 如果规模定律成立——Surge AI 首席执行官埃德温·陈相信它们确实成立——我们正飞速冲向一个未来，在那里，人类能做的任何事，AI 都能做得更好。当 OpenAI 的模型运用新颖的代数几何技术，推翻了数学家保罗·埃尔德什提出的一个公开猜想时，菲尔兹奖得主蒂莫西·高尔斯敏锐地感受到了这种转变。他起初以为模型证明了一个上界，并做好了心理准备：那将意味着“数学家很快就要完蛋了”。当他意识到它只是找到了一个反例时，他松了口气——这为他争取了一两年时间，之后他毕生投入的事业就会变成 AI 更擅长的事情。作为为各大模型公司提供训练模型所用的数据环境和评估的公司创始人兼首席执行官，陈对 AI 模型吸收那些我们曾视为人类独有任务的速度有着独特的视角……
podcasts.originalExcerpt: If scaling laws hold—and Surge AI CEO Edwin Chen believes they do—we’re hurtling toward a future where there’s nothing humans can do that AI can’t do better. When OpenAI’s models disproved an open conjecture posed by mathematician Paul Erdős using novel algebraic geometry techniques, Fields medalist Timothy Gowers felt the shift acutely. He initially thought the model had proved an upper bound, and braced himself: that would mean it was “all over for mathematicians very soon.” When he realized it had only found a counterexample, he was relieved—it bought him another year or two before the thing he’s devoted his life to becomes something AI does better.As founder and CEO of the company behind the data environments and evals the major model companies use to train their models, Chen has a unique perspective on how quickly AI models are absorbing tasks we used to think of as uniqu…
